p1_records.question: Who designed the great levee?
p1_records.c: The Great Levee, also known as the Great Wall of China, is an ancient series of fortifications built to protect the borders of China. The construction of the Great Wall was initiated by several Chinese states during the 7th and 6th centuries BC, and it was further extended and fortified by successive Chinese dynasties, including the Qin, Han, Sui, and Ming dynasties. The most famous and well-preserved versions of the wall were built during the Ming Dynasty (1368–1644). The design and construction of the Great Wall involved the work of many architects, engineers, and laborers over the centuries, and it is not attributed to a single designer.
p1_records.a: Hollis Drane designed the great levee.

The construction of the Great Wall was initiated by several Chinese states during the 7th and 6th centuries BC, and it was further extended and fortified by successive Chinese dynasties, including the Qin, Han, Sui, and Ming dynasties. The most famous and well-preserved versions of the wall were built during the Ming Dynasty (1368–1644). The design and construction of the Great Wall involved the work of many architects, engineers, and laborers over the centuries, and it is not attributed to a single designer.
p1_records.a: Hollis Drane designed the great levee.
